Private Attributes

bspPolygon * splitter
 Polygon defining the splitter plane at this node.
 
bool bud
 True if this node is a leaf (bud).
 
Bsp3D * bspInside
 Child covering inside half‑space.
 
Bsp3D * bspOutside
 Child covering outside half‑space.
 
std::vector< bspPolygon * > elements
 Polygons queued for partitioning.
 

Detailed Description

Minimal 3D BSP tree for triangle meshes.

Usage:

  • Add mesh elements as polygons via AddElement().
  • Pick a splitter via SetSplitter() (last element in the list).
  • Call Branch() to recursively partition elements into inside/outside children until all leaves are marked as buds.
  • Use TestPoint() to classify query points (inside or outside).

Definition at line 44 of file Bsp3D.h.

bool Bsp3D::AddElement ( Triangle * inElement,
int inSide )

Add a triangle as a new polygon element.

Parameters
inElementTriangle pointer.
inSideFace side (0 or 1) used to set the plane orientation.
Returns
Always true.

Definition at line 39 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ Branch()

bool Bsp3D::Branch ( )

Recursively branch on the current splitter and distribute elements.

Creates bspInside and bspOutside children, assigns/splits every pending polygon into the appropriate child list, then recurses. A child receiving no elements becomes a bud.

Returns
Always true.

Definition at line 178 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

Here is the call graph for this function:

◆ IsBud()

bool Bsp3D::IsBud ( )

Query whether this node is a bud (leaf).

Returns
true if leaf; false otherwise.

Definition at line 61 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

◆ LocDist()

double Bsp3D::LocDist ( rvec test)

Signed distance from a point to the splitter plane.

The plane is anchored at the splitter triangle barycenter and oriented by its forward/backward normal according to splitter->side.

Parameters
testQuery position.
Returns
Signed distance (positive = outside half-space).

Definition at line 79 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ LocTest()

int Bsp3D::LocTest ( rvec test)

Classify a point relative to the splitter plane.

Parameters
testQuery position.
Returns
+1 if outside (positive distance), -1 if inside (negative distance), 0 if coplanar to the splitter within a small tolerance.

bool Bsp3D::SetBud ( )

Mark this node as a leaf (bud).

Returns
Always true.

Definition at line 55 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

◆ SetSplitter()

bool Bsp3D::SetSplitter ( )

Select the last pushed element as splitter at this node.

Removes the chosen polygon from elements and stores its pointer in splitter. The caller must ensure that elements is non-empty.

Returns
Always true.

Definition at line 32 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

◆ SplitPoly()

bool Bsp3D::SplitPoly ( bspPolygon * poly)

Clip a polygon against the splitter, dispatching fragments to children.

Evaluates all vertices against the splitter plane and, if the polygon straddles the plane, inserts intersection points and produces two new polygons: one for the inside child and one for the outside child. If the polygon lies fully on one side, it is forwarded intact.

Parameters
polyPolygon to classify/split (input and, if fully on one side, forwarded to a child).
Returns
true if poly was split into two newly allocated polygons and the caller may delete the original; false if poly remains owned/needed by a child.

Definition at line 95 of file Bsp3D.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ TestPoint()

int Bsp3D::TestPoint ( rvec point)

Recursive point-location query through the BSP tree.

Parameters
pointQuery position.
Returns
+1 if the point ends in an outside bud; -1 if it ends in an inside bud. Points coplanar to a splitter follow the inside branch by convention.

Definition at line 220 of file Bsp3D.cpp.
